methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $39M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Addionics is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Moshiel Biton and Farid Tariq that has raised $39M+ to date. The peer set comprises five private companies at identical stage and sector (Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, CarbonCapture Inc., and Cemvita), supplemented by one public comparable, providing a robust basis for triangulation.

Two methods apply with material divergence. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to $39M total raised and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yields $74M. Peer-median valuation across the five same-stage, same-sector comparables stands at $310M, with a range spanning $120M (Cemvita) to $500M (Pano AI). This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneous technology maturity and capital efficiency within the climate-tech cohort.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $63M to $356M, with a median of $192M. The material spread between stage-progression ($74M) and peer median ($310M) reflects uncertainty around whether Addionics' technology, market position, and capital efficiency align with the higher-end or lower-end peers in the sample. Key uncertainties include technology de-risking progress relative to comparables, customer traction, and cost-of-capital trends in climate technology. Additional data on revenue, burn rate, or specific technical milestones would narrow this range meaningfully.
